brief_justification: This is a theoretical paper that proposes a conceptual and methodological framework for analyzing transnational social movements, using the World Social Forum as an illustrative case. It contains no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, no tables or figures, and relies entirely on theoretical argumentation drawing from political theory (Habermas, Rawls, Arendt, Touraine) and social movement literature. The paper discusses globalization as a political and cultural struggle and argues that transnational social movements represent a new form of political subject. It fits International Relations given its focus on transnational politics and world governance beyond nation-states.
